verdict.answer_first: B states the answer immediately: "Start by making the error observable"; A starts useful too but spends sentence 1 framing the issue before the ordered fix.
verdict.decisiveness: A commits to a clearer sequence: "Get the actual exception first" and "Plot the 500 rate per host/instance"; B gives a strong first move but then surveys categories.
verdict.fabrication: Both make unsupported frequency claims: A says "almost always" and "usually collapses"; B says "single most common cause" without evidence.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to execute because it gives an ordered checklist and "suggested first two moves" instead of only categories after logging.
